Overwhelmingly faithful, native, and consistent (core terms correct: Dichtematrix, positiv-semidefinit, hermitesch, reine Zustände, Spur/Tr, Standardbasismessung, globale Phase; du-register held throughout, e.g. "Beachte insbesondere"). One isolated semantic drift: line 134 renders the source's "Matrices of this form, in addition to being density matrices, are always projections" as "Matrizen dieser Form sind neben der positiven Semidefinitheit stets Projektionen" — the qualifier "being density matrices" was silently swapped for "der positiven Semidefinitheit," a property not named in the source at that point. The substituted claim is still true of these matrices, so it does not seriously mislead, but it changes what the sentence asserts and drops the intended point that these rank-1 objects are themselves density matrices. Everything else, including the whole final third (pure states, eigenvalues, global-phase degeneracy, faithful representation), tracks the English exactly.

examples.source: Matrices of this form, in addition to being density matrices, are always projections and have rank equal to 1.
examples.translation: Matrizen dieser Form sind neben der positiven Semidefinitheit stets Projektionen und haben Rang gleich 1.

examples.why: The source qualifier 'in addition to being density matrices' was replaced with 'neben der positiven Semidefinitheit' (in addition to positive semidefiniteness), inserting a property the source did not cite here and dropping the intended statement that these matrices are density matrices.

editor_note: Faithful, native technical prose; core terms correct and consistent (valor esperado, función de costo, hamiltoniano, ansatz, puertas swap, sesión de Runtime), informal "tú" register held throughout. Note two non-meaning artifacts outside this run's scope: line 265 leaves the English "Estimator's built-in resilience..." bullet undeleted directly above its Spanish translation on line 266 (a duplication/leak, correct Spanish still present, not misleading), and a spelling slip "menciónó" on line 39.

Native, fluent, terminologically consistent technical prose (Operator/Erwartungswert/Eigenzustand/Variationsform/Strafterm/gegenseitig orthogonal), informal "du" held throughout, and meaning-faithful across the VQE/SSVQE/VQD/QSR sections including the subtle induction on weight-priority and the increasing-eigenvalue sanity caveat. The one point worth recording: at line 441 the German inserts a negation — "die Transpilierung nicht ... eingebaut wurde" (transpilation was NOT built in) — where the English source (line 443) literally reads "transpilation has been built in." The EN source is self-contradictory (it then invites the reader to build transpilation in, and neither function contains transpilation code), so the German is in fact the CORRECT reading and matches the code + the next sentence. Do NOT "fix" this back to match the buggy English; the "nicht" should stay. Not misleading; flagged only because the rendering diverges verbatim from source and merits one human confirmation.

examples.why: German inverts the literal source negation, but the source is self-contradictory and the German is the factually correct reading — does not mislead the learner. Recorded so a human confirms and does not revert it to the erroneous EN wording.

The bulk of the file is faithful, fluent technical Tagalog with correct, consistent core terms (qubit/gate/circuit/superposition/eigenvalue all kept in English, Hamiltonian and controlled-U handled well). But there is a genuine mistranslation that misleads the learner: the mathematical term "odd" (as in odd binary number, i.e. ending in 1) is rendered "kakaiba," which in Tagalog means "strange/unusual," not the number-parity sense (gansal). This appears in both halves of the second Check-your-understanding exercise ("peaks on every odd binary number" and its answer), gutting the exercise — a student reads "peaks on every strange binary number." Note the file elsewhere keeps "even" in English (line 390/404), so the parity pair is also inconsistent. Second, smaller drift: "That signal will be very broad in Fourier space" becomes "napakalalim" (very deep) instead of broad/malawak, undercutting the width/inverse-width pedagogical point (the same sentence correctly used "malawak" for the frequency range just before). One awkward coinage "ipamagkuha" (line 571) for "might suggest" is not a real word but does not itself mislead.

examples.source: What computational state superposition would correspond to a QFT with peaks on every odd binary number? / peaks on every odd binary numbered state
examples.translation: ...may mga tuktok sa bawat kakaibang binary number ... makakakita ka ng mga tuktok sa bawat kakaibang binary numbered state
examples.suggested: ...sa bawat gansal na binary number ... sa bawat gansal (odd) na binary numbered state
examples.why: 'odd' (parity, 2k+1) is mistranslated as 'kakaiba' = strange/unusual. This breaks the whole check-your-understanding exercise about which basis states get peaks; a learner cannot recover the intended odd/even distinction, especially since 'even' is left in English nearby.

examples.source: That signal will be very broad in Fourier space.
examples.translation: Ang signal na iyon ay magiging napakalalim sa Fourier space.
examples.suggested: Ang signal na iyon ay magiging napakalawak sa Fourier space.
examples.why: 'broad' rendered as 'malalim' (deep) instead of 'malawak' (wide/broad); the point is spectral WIDTH (short pulse -> wide frequency spread), so 'deep' loses the intended intuition. The same sentence correctly used 'malawak' for 'broad range of frequencies'.

examples.why: 'ipamagkuha' is not a real Tagalog word; a native technical writer would use 'ipahiwatig' (suggest/hint). Awkward but not itself misleading.

Native, faithful, well-edited prose with consistent informal "ти" register (Дотримуйся, Уникай, Захищай); all security instructions preserved accurately. One isolated terminology slip: on line 52 "a temporary credential" (describing a bearer token) is rendered "тимчасовий обліковий запис" = "a temporary account", which is conceptually wrong (a token is not an account) and inconsistent with line 24 where "credentials" is correctly translated "облікові дані". It does not mislead about the actual instruction (the one-hour expiry and regenerate-a-new-one guidance is intact), so this is a minor slip, not a semantic failure.

examples.source: A _bearer token_ is a temporary credential that expires after no more than one hour.
examples.translation: _Bearer-токен_ — це тимчасовий обліковий запис, термін дії якого закінчується не більш ніж через одну годину.
examples.suggested: _Bearer-токен_ — це тимчасові облікові дані (тимчасовий доступ), термін дії яких закінчується не більш ніж через одну годину.
examples.why: 'credential' is mistranslated as 'обліковий запис' (account); a bearer token is a credential/access token, not an account, and 'облікові дані' is already the correct rendering used on line 24.

editor_note: Native, faithful technical prose; core terms correct and consistent (observable→спостережувана, expectation value→очікуване значення, transpile→транспіляція, gate→гейт, circuit→квантова схема), informal "ти" register held throughout, and meaning-critical passages preserved (check-understanding answers a→Estimator/b→Sampler; the SQD sequential caveats about charge conservation and convergence in "Навіщо обидві?" are fully intact). Only nit: "sample a state" rendered "вибирати стан" (select), but the next sentence disambiguates and it does not mislead.

Faithful, native technical Tagalog; meaning preserved paragraph-for-paragraph including the delicate negations (e.g. line ~609 "Tanging sa qubit 6... hindi nag-c-commute ang Z error, habang nag-c-commute ang X error" correctly renders "does a Z error fail to commute, while an X error does commute") and the Step 4 post-selection discard condition. Domain terms consistently kept in English (qubit, observable, forward/backward bounds, sampling overhead), informal register throughout with no po/opo slips. Only a trivial naturalness note (not misleading): "kung gaano sila katumbas na i-mitigate" is a slightly awkward gloss of "how worthwhile they are to mitigate".

Meaning-faithful and native across all sections; core terms correct and the pedagogy tracks the English. Verified the load-bearing caveats survive: the no-signaling "faster-than-light" clause (hindi maaaring depende sa kung sinukat din o hindi ang Y... komunikasyong mas mabilis kaysa sa liwanag), the "collapses only as far as required" nuance, entanglement=correlation for state vectors, and the reverse/inverse conjugate-transpose statement. All probabilities/outcomes (2/3, 1/3, 3/7, 2/7; GHZ→|000>/|111>, W→|psi^+>) match, no inversions or hallucinations. Only a benign stylistic seam (not misleading): the first half keeps "quantum state"/"classical state set" in English while from line 303 on it renders "quantum na estado"/"klasikal na estado" and measurement as "sukat" — consistency territory the run deprioritizes. Informal register (tayo/natin) holds throughout.

Two pervasive domain-terminology mistranslations. (1) "destructive / non-destructive measurement" is rendered as "розкладальне / нерозкладальне" (decomposable / non-decomposable) throughout — including the section heading and the definitional sentence at line 162 — instead of the correct "руйнівне / неруйнівне вимірювання" (destroying/non-destroying); "розкладальний" derives from розкладати=decompose, a different concept, so the entire "Non-destructive measurements" section teaches the concept under a wrong name and the explanation "refers to the system being measured but not necessarily its state" no longer fits. (2) "traced out" (the partial-trace operation, i.e. discarding a subsystem) is rendered as "відслідковується" = "is being tracked/monitored" at lines 35, 39, and 175 ("не відслідковується" for "not traced out"), a near-opposite connotation. The remaining prose is faithful, natural, and correctly informal.

examples.source: So far in the lesson, we've concerned ourselves with *destructive* measurements
examples.translation: До цього моменту в уроці ми розглядали *розкладальні* вимірювання
examples.suggested: ...*руйнівні* вимірювання
examples.why: 'Розкладальні' means 'decomposable', not 'destructive'; the correct quantum term is 'руйнівні'. Used pervasively, including the section heading 'Нерозкладальні вимірювання'.

examples.source: can imagine that it is traced out
examples.translation: можна вважати, що вона відслідковується
examples.suggested: можна вважати, що вона виводиться слідом / трасується
examples.why: 'Trace out' is the partial-trace (discard) operation; 'відслідковується' means 'is being tracked/monitored', reversing the intent. Also at lines 39, 175.

examples.source: where the system X is not traced out, but is part of the output
examples.translation: де система X не відслідковується, а є частиною виходу
examples.suggested: де система X не виводиться слідом, а є частиною виходу
examples.why: Same 'trace out'→'track' error; a learner reads that X is 'not monitored' rather than 'not discarded via partial trace'.

editor_note: Faithful, native technical prose with no semantic drift; core terms correct and consistent (expectation values→очікувані значення, observables→спостережувані, error mitigation→пом'якшення помилок, "без шкоди для продуктивності" for "without sacrificing performance"), and the informal "ти" imperative register holds throughout the Recommendations list (Дізнайся, Переглянь, Попрактикуйся).

Faithful, native technical prose; all quantitative/conditional claims preserved — threshold inequality "Якщо $p$ менше за $1/C$... зниження рівня помилок", Shor "перевищує приблизно 3,23%", and the load-bearing doubly-vs-singly-exponential contrast ("подвійно експоненційним" vs "одиночно експоненційно"). Core terms consistent (gate→вентиль, gadgets→гаджети, fault-tolerant→відмовостійка, concatenate→конкатенувати, Steane code→код Стіна); informal "ти" register holds ("Зверни увагу", "Твій відгук"). No drift, no hallucination, caveats intact.

Meaning-faithful throughout with no drift or hallucination; the delicate analytical passages survive intact — the mirror-circuit caveats ("вона вдвічі збільшує кількість гейтів (що перебільшує ефект шуму), і вона може недооцінювати певні помилки, коли шум симетрично скасовується через межу дзеркала"), the "not representative / not a verdict" warning about the single 26-qubit tfim circuit (line 1372), and Rustiq's outlier-skewed averages are all rendered accurately. Register is consistently informal "ти" (ти зрозумієш, переконайся, Пам'ятай). Core terms are consistent (транспілер, гейт, схема, fidelity→точність, менеджер проходів, компонування/маршрутизація). One benign observation for the terminology sweep: qubit is rendered via the stem "квіт" (двоквітна глибина, квітів) rather than the more standard "кубіт", but it is used 100% consistently and is unambiguous in context, so it does not mislead.

examples.source: Two-qubit depth: The depth of the circuit counting only two-qubit gates.
examples.translation: Двоквітна глибина: Глибина схеми з урахуванням лише двоквітних гейтів.
examples.suggested: consider кубіт-based rendering (двокубітна) if the project glossary prefers кубіт
examples.why: qubit is rendered via the stem "квіт" rather than the more conventional Ukrainian "кубіт"; consistent and understandable in context, so non-misleading, but flagged for the deterministic terminology sweep
